characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: Adaptational Nice Girl: Downplayed, while Alison like Sam, is in general very friendly and kind, Samantha has a lot more patience when it comes to the ghosts than her British counterpart as well as being much more likely to cater to the ghosts' demands. Though in fairness Sam has a much easier in getting her business up and running as well as having another paying job which removes a big source of stress that tested Alison's patience, while in turn the Woodstone ghosts are also much nicer and more understanding than their British counterparts. All for Nothing: Sam's investigation into Alberta's murder and the podcast surrounding it turn out to big waste of time in "Whodunnit" because Hetty knew who it was the whole time and it wasn't any of the suspects Sam came up with. Blessed with Suck: She's able to communicate with the dead. Unfortunately, they're like a group of demanding toddlers, each vying for her attention over the others.Her "power" makes her unable to live in large cities, as the sheer number of ghosts in more populated areas like New York City is absolutely overwhelming.She also can't always tell whether someone's a ghost or alive. Her "power" makes her unable to live in large cities, as the sheer number of ghosts in more populated areas like New York City is absolutely overwhelming. She also can't always tell whether someone's a ghost or alive.

characters.name: Samantha "Sam" Arondekar
characters.description: Adaptational Nice Girl: Downplayed, while Alison like Sam, is in general very friendly and kind, Samantha has a lot more patience when it comes to the ghosts than her British counterpart as well as being much more likely to cater to the ghosts' demands. Though in fairness Sam has a much easier in getting her business up and running as well as having another paying job which removes a big source of stress that tested Alison's patience, while in turn the Woodstone ghosts are also much nicer and more understanding than their British counterparts. All for Nothing: Sam's investigation into Alberta's murder and the podcast surrounding it turn out to big waste of time in "Whodunnit" because Hetty knew who it was the whole time and it wasn't any of the suspects Sam came up with. Blessed with Suck: She's able to communicate with the dead. Unfortunately, they're like a group of demanding toddlers, each vying for her attention over the others.Her "power" makes her unable to live in large cities, as the sheer number of ghosts in more populated areas like New York City is absolutely overwhelming.She also can't always tell whether someone's a ghost or alive. Her "power" makes her unable to live in large cities, as the sheer number of ghosts in more populated areas like New York City is absolutely overwhelming. She also can't always tell whether someone's a ghost or alive.

characters.name: Jay Arondekar
characters.description: Adaptational Intelligence: Never quite reaches Mike's occasionalToo Dumb to Livemoments. All for Nothing: As with Sam, Jay's involvement in the investigation of Alberta's murder and the podcast turns out to be a waste of time because "Whodunnit" reveals that Hetty knew who it was the entire time, and it wasn't any of the suspects they had. Bonus time wasted points for Jay because he wasted 8+ hours putting together a murder board with all the evidence and suspects. Big Eater: He can put away large amounts of snack food very quickly — such as a big packet of Oreos and multiple cupcakes — and him being a professional chef feeds this obsession. He tries to lie about it to Sam, but the ghosts always rat him out.Trevor:This is not a man destined to keep it tight, Sam. Culturally Religious: In Season 3, it's revealed in passing that he's a Hindu, but he doesn't seem to be particularly devout. Deal with the Devil:An inadvertent example. In Season 4, he hires a publicist and quickly sees his cooking get him the recognition he deserves in major magazines. Unfortunately for him his publicist is Elias, who's on a mission to gather souls for hell and sets about trying to kill him to collect it as soon as possible.

characters.name: The Farnsbys
characters.description: Adaptational Jerkass: Barclay and Bunny were not particularly mean people and the worst Barclay has done is talk a lot in front of Alison and Mike. Henry and Margaret are openly antagonistic to Sam and Jay, wanting them to leave. Dirty Old Man: As revealed in "The Baby Bjorn", they host orgies once a month. Hate Sink: All the arrogant classism of Hetty and Isaac with none of the good intentions, they twice try to prevent Sam and Jay from opening the B & B, partly because they want no noise whatsoever and partly just because of a sheer hatred of the middle-class. It says a lot that the only time they were truly nice to Sam and Jay was when they thought that Sam and Jay wanted in on their orgies. Hypocritical Humor: They are both pretty snooty and arrogant individuals who deride the Arondekers for opening up an Air B&B for the “middle-class”, claiming the guests will be noisy and unwanted pests on the property. Keep in mind this is coming from the same people that host orgies in their family home once a month and film videos of their sexual deviancy for perverted pleasure, ironically showing they’re more degenerates than the people they’re trying to keep out. Nosy Neighbor: The both of them try to prevent Sam and Jay from opening the B and B.

characters.name: Bela Arondekar
characters.description: Adaptational Nice Girl: Overall, she's much nicer to Jay and treats him with more respect compared to Mike's sisters, though the two get into arguments sometimes. All Girls Want Bad Boys: Brings a friend, Eric, to the Woodstone for Christmas, but wants Trevor to possess him to have time with a "bad boy." After Eric dies for a short time, she seeshimin a new light. Buxom Beauty Standard: In "Jay's Sister", whenSam is conveying Trevor's goodbye message to Bela, he mentions how he likes Bela's big boobs, butSam refuses to say that bit even after he protests.Trevor:You forgot the boobs.Sam:Nope. Just keep going. Catfishing: Was in an online relationship with whom she thought was a corporate banker in New York. Turns out to have been Trevor, who used the only picture of him that was ever placed online: his obituary picture. Foolish Sibling, Responsible Sibling: She's the foolish to Jay's responsible. She has a history of bad relationships and once left her car in a private parking lot long enough for them to put a boot on it.

characters.name: Freddie
characters.description: Butt-Monkey: Ghosts make his life hell on two occasions, manipulating Sam and Jay into disciplining him only for them to apologize for their mistakes.In "The Perfect Assistant" Jessica, who haunts his car, fabricates a story that Freddie killed her so he wouldn't retain his new job because she enjoys his life delivering food and seeing different places. He is let go until Sam and Jay learn the truth and make a grand gesture of amends.In "The Family Business" Hetty dislikes Sam's growing friendly attitude with "the help" and with Trevor's help sabotages the website that Freddie revamped. This causes Sam to blow up at him in the main hall before the customers who are frustrated over this perceived screw-up as well. He leaves but comes back when they apologize and tell him somehow the registration laptop got accessed well past when he left and they know it wasn't him. In "The Perfect Assistant" Jessica, who haunts his car, fabricates a story that Freddie killed her so he wouldn't retain his new job because she enjoys his life delivering food and seeing different places. He is let go until Sam and Jay learn the truth and make a grand gesture of amends. In "The Family Business" Hetty dislikes Sam's growing friendly attitude with "the help" and with Trevor's help sabotages the website that Freddie revamped. This causes Sam to blow up at him in the main hall before the customers who are frustrated over this perceived screw-up as well. He leaves but comes back when they apologize and tell him somehow the registration laptop got accessed well past when he left and they know it wasn't him. Chekhov's Gun: In "The Family Business" he installs a security camera over the check-in desk and at the end of the episode notices in a recording of when the system got sabotaged the keys seemed to move on their own. Promoted Fanboy:In-Universe. He has a dream of running his own B&B and happily works for Sam and Jay, despite the two misunderstandings that cause his life serious stress.

characters.name: Mark
characters.description: Collector of the Strange: Collects expensive sneakers with Jay, who allows him to store his collection at the mansion. Limited Social Circle: The fact that Sam and Jay invite him to parties and he attends them is a sign of just how limited the social lives of both parties are. Serious Business: He may be Sam and Jay's contractor & business partner, butneverinvade his space when he is part of the community theater group. Ever!

characters.name: Kyle
characters.description: Commonality Connection: He bonds with Sam over their shared ability to see ghosts and how it affects their daily lives, which arouses suspicion and jealousy in Jay, who believes Kyle has feelings for Sam, although Sam reassures him there is nothing going on between them.Unfortunately, Jay turns out to be right as Kyle misreads the signals on this and tries to make a move on her, which the alreadyHappily MarriedSam does not appreciate. The Friend Nobody Likes: Jay utterly despises the man for trying to sleep with his wife, only relenting when the Ghosts blackmail him with hisBig Eatertendencies (four double deckers from Sonic for breakfast). Sam doesn't like him either, only reluctantly calling him up. Gender Flip: In the UK version, the only other person Alison met who could see ghosts was a woman, specifically pop starKylie Minogue. Hellhole Prison: The prison was actually normal (which by itself is not great), but any prison is this when you can see ghosts.Kyle: Not a lot of prisoners die peacefully in their sleep. Mirror Character: Kyle's life is what Sam's might have been if she didn't have a supportive husband or a secluded home with reasonably friendly ghosts.

characters.name: Thorfinn
characters.description: Adaptational Dumbass: Robin was theOnly Sane Manamong the ghosts and despite being a neanderthal, was surprisingly intelligent. Thor is far more prone toComically Missing the PointandMurder Is the Best Solution. Ambiguously Bi: When Pete is listing Isaac’s romantic options, he includes, "I don’t know what Thor means when he talks about 'boat rules,' but that sounds…", implying that Thor considers men an option under certain circumstances. He also admires one of the lumberjack videos that Bela watches and the stripper Sam hires for Isaac's bachelor party. Age Lift: Robin, his UK counterpart, has been dead for around 10,000 years or more, while in contrast Thorfinn died a thousand years ago. Berserk Button: Danish people. Thorfinn spent a lot of his adult life murdering Danes due to Norse's prejudices. A thousand years later, he still hates Danes and tells many stories about dismembering them with pride. The fact that his son Bjorn married and fathered children with one nearly causes Thor to disown him. Bruiser with a Soft Center: He's a big guy with a love of fighting (heisa Viking, after all), but he's ultimately a good-natured person who is loyal to his friends and capable of being very sensitive.

characters.name: Sasappis
characters.description: Ambiguously Bi: He briefly dates a woman in Jay's dreams, before "her" interests make it clear that, naturally, she's just part of Jay's subconscious. Though that realization ends the relationship, he admits the romantic feelings for Jay were genuine. Best Friend: With Thorfinn. Sass was Thor's first friend in the 500 years after Thor died and have remained close in the 500 years since Sass' own death. Braids, Beads and Buckskins: He is dressed in this manner. SomewhatJustified, since Native Americans actually dressed like that in the period Sasappis lived, though certain details of his costume are inaccurate to the Lenape specifically (namely his hair, which is longer than was typical for Lenape men of the time, although the style is about as accurate as production could be without forcing Romàn Zaragoza to cut off his natural long locks; the costume is otherwise very accurate thanks to the production having hired a Lenape consultant). Comedic Sociopathy: Sass is far from a sociopath, but he loves drama thanks to it breaking up the monotony of being a ghost. At one point he even goes so far as to thank Hetty for planning a party purely to upstage Nigel because of the entertainment the fight might cause. A Day in the Limelight:He gets some focus in "Flower's Article" when Sam runs into the ghost of a Lenape woman he was attracted to in life, and while working on her article Sam delivers messages back and forth between the two. In the end, he realizes that the two never actually knew each other all that well. Then immediately changes his tune to anger, after learning that Sam's forwardness on "his" behalf is what actually turned his crush off."Ghostwriter" details his history, as he wanted to be his tribe's storyteller, but his father, who had tried and failed, worried his son would follow in his failure. When Sam starts to show the same fears of failure concerning the B&B and the website, Sasappis reveals he feared it too, thanks to his father's words, but reveals h

characters.name: Capt. Isaac Higgintoot (né Higginbottom)
characters.description: Accidental Murder: In 1777, he used a gun's scope to admire Nigel from afar. When he sneezed, he accidentally pulled the trigger. Affectionate Nickname: His wife Beatrice gave him the nickname "Cricket" and had a dead cricket sewn onto a napkin before he died. It's now part of his ghost being. All Gays Love Theatre: He states that musical theatre is a passion of his and desperately wants a Broadway musical about him, just likeAlexander Hamilton. The Beard: In life, he was married to Beatrice despite the fact that he was gay. Justified as being gay in Colonial America was punishable by death. In death, he feels guilty that he ruined Beatrice’s life because he was gay and therefore was not able to love her. Cannot Spit It Out: Try as he does, he struggles to admit first his sexuality, then his crush on Nigel; it takes the majority of Season 1 for him to reveal his feelings to Nigel himself.

characters.name: Henrietta "Hetty" Woodstone
characters.description: Adaptational Angst Downgrade: Only intwoaspects as her marriage was arguably worse than Fanny's overall. Hetty wasn't murdered by Elias after discovering an affair and since Elias wasn't aDepraved Homosexuallike George, she never developed Fanny's homophobia. Adaptational Nice Girl: While still as elitist as Fanny, she's less obnoxious about it than her British counterpart and much more accepting of Samantha as her descendant, than Fanny is of Alison. Age Lift: Fanny was in her late 50s when she died while Hetty is in her 40s. Awesome, but Impractical: Her owning a telephone in life reeks of this. Hetty proudly sent letters bragging about owning one... but due to the fact that it was at a time when very few even exist (her number was literally 4) to the point no one she knew even had one, it was effectively useless. Awful Wedded Life: Her husband made their marriageAll About Him.

characters.name: Alberta Haynes (née Esther Greene)
characters.description: Beautiful Singing Voice: She was a jazz singer in her day, with a beautiful voice, so much so it became her power as a ghost, as her humming can echo through the mansion that the living can hear her. After gaining attention from Sam's podcast about her death, people seeking out her albums all admit she was a very talented vocalist. Big Beautiful Woman: She's a full-figured, attractive woman, who in death has caught the attention of Pete and a poltergeist who visits Woodstone in Season 3. Deconstructed in "Alberta's Podcast", where she reveals, for all her beauty, hard work, and talent, fat women had ahellof a time trying to make it show business in her day, and she was frequently ignored in favor of skinnier women who weren't nearly as good. She eventually got so frustrated with being passed overthat she resorted to outright sabotageso she could finally get her chance. Composite Character: Because it was fitting for her character, Alberta gotJemima the Plague victim child ghost's ability to communicate with the living by singing when the latter's counterpart gotAdapted Out. She also physically resembles Kitty in the UK version, being aBig Beautiful Womanof color, though Flower has a lot more of Kitty's naïveté. She also shares a bit of Thomas’ back story as they were killed because they were in the way of the person their murderer desired. Like Thomas, she helps Sam with her drama skills for an upcoming show. A Day in the Limelight:We learn more about her in "Alberta’s Fan" thanks to a jazz historian."Alberta's Podcast" explores how she rose to fame and introduces a suspect to her murder.Her bootlegging experience comes in handy in “The Liquor License” as Sam and Jay have to run a speakeasy to avoid being caught by the inspector because they don’t have a liquor license.She finds out she has a great-grandniece in “Alberta’s Descendant”. At the end of the episode, another suspect to her murder is introduced after Sam and Jay worked out that the person being referred t

characters.name: Flower (Susan Montero)
characters.description: Ambiguously Bi: Implied when she bemoaned about howthe other two members of her throupleonce made love without her. Ascended to a Higher Plane of Existence: The Season 3 premiere reveals her to have been the one who moved on...or not, as the Halloween episode ends with her voice coming from the bottom of the well. Attention Deficit... Ooh, Shiny!: She has a rather short attention span and is easily distracted by things that pass by her line of sight. This is mainly due to the hallucinogenics swilling inside her system which gave her a perpetual drug-filled daze in the afterlife. Aww Look They Really Do Love Each Other: Despite at times being somewhat annoyed with her (apart from Thor, her boyfriend), all of the ghosts are heartbroken when they think she's been sucked off. Even the cholera ghosts are distraught, due to Flower being the only one of the main ghosts to spend time with and sincerely befriend them. Bears Are Bad News: Flower died from being mauled by a bear.

characters.name: Peter "Pete" Martino
characters.description: Adaptation Relationship Overhaul: He isn'tHeterosexual Life-Partnerswith Isaac to the degree Pat was with the Captain, instead being best friends with Jay. Ambiguously Bi: In "The Liquor License" after Jay explains his plan, he openly states that he's a little turned on by him. He seems to get very jealous when Jay hangs out with Mark instead. When Sasappis briefly dates a (female-bodied) figment of Jay's subconscious in his dreams, Pete wants to know what it's like to date Jay. After Alberta and Hetty have a falling out, Pete says he's ready to objectify Jason Momoa with Alberta in Hetty's place. Annoying Arrows: He cheerily exists in death with the arrow that killed him still embedded in his neck, though he does mention that the thing is constantly itching him because the fiberglass shaft rubs against his flesh. Very much averted however in his backstory, since the arrow didkillhim. Berserk Button: He gets a bit touchy when people, like Jay, boil him down to just being the "Arrow Guy", as he was many other attributes and qualities that he'd rather be known for. Beware the Nice Ones: He reveals in season 4 that he's actually a massive fan of bloodsport due to repressed rage and in season 5 that he enjoys watching the basement ghosts' fight club. Each of these revelations leave those who hear them visibly disturbed.

characters.name: Trevor Lefkowitz
characters.description: Adaptational Nice Guy: Overall, he's a lot more pleasant than Julian ever was. Whereas Julian outright tried to murder Alison by pushing her out a window, Trevor causing Sam's fall was an accident, as he was only trying to scare her away. More so, while Julian,who openly preached family values to parliament, died in the midst of sexual debauchery while neglecting his wife and daughter, on Christmas no less, Trevor merely made up his sexual exploits to save face with his cruel-hearted friends, when actually doing an act of kindness towards the young associate they were trying to haze. He also takes on Thomas's crush on the living who can see them, but with Trevor it's just mild, at worst inappropriate, flirtation, as opposed to Thomas's delusional obsession. And even by the end of the first season, he happily considers Sam a true friend. Big Brother Instinct: When his brother Jeremyused a loopholeto stay at Woodstone for free, Trevor was thrilled to get to spend time with him, until he learned that Jeremy was hiding out at Woodstone to avoid taking over the family business because he didn't feel he was good enough. As much as he missed his brother, he helped Sam and Jay toss him out for his own good. Broken Pedestal: In the span of two days he finds out that his father cheated on his mother and his childhood hero, Mel Gibson, is an anti-Semite. Chivalrous Pervert: He's a horndog who shamelessly hits on Sam and makes crude remarks about the women he sees on TV and the Internet. However, he absolutely refuses to try anything with Stephanie (despite her constantly coming on to him) because she was still biologically underage when she died, and he briefly stops hitting on Sam when he starts hooking up with Hetty out of respect for their relationship. Composite Character:He takes on Thomas Thorne's crush on the living female main character.Like Julian, he is pantless, loves wild parties, and can move objects in the living world.He takes Sir Humphrey's Body's role in havin

characters.name: Carol Martino
characters.description: Adaptational Expansion: Carol's marriage to Pete is explored much more than Pat and his Carol, down to revealing that an argument over donut holes is behind Pete's death. She even becomes a ghost after she suddenlydies from choking on a donut hole. Adaptational Sympathy:Inverted. The only way she differed from the UK Carol before becoming a ghost was that the British Carol cheated on Pat because he was an extremeCreature of Habit(for example, he refused to take her to a new Italian restaurant because it was fish and chips night), while this Carol had no such excuse. Ambiguously Innocent: Carol cheated on her husband and tacitly supported her mob family. For example, It’s not particularly moral to abet the mob in running a business dry because you feel the need to maintain a code of silence. Especially, when you’re dead and can suffer no ill-consequences from it. But Carol, didn’t think she was doing anything wrong in those instances. Ascended Extra: Unlike the British Carol, the American Carol makes much more of a recurring role here down to dying and becoming a prominent ghost in the show. Ascended to a Higher Plane of Existence:After taking the deal to save Jay's soul in Pete's place, she briefly "goes down" on everyone, but because of her selflessness, she instead is pulled out hell and is "sucked off" to heaven.

characters.name: Crash
characters.description: Age Lift: A double example. Not only is he physically younger than his UK counterpart Humphrey, but he died in the 1950s while Humphrey in the 16th century. Ambiguously Bi: In "Trevor's Pants", Flower asks him to be in a polyamorous relationship with herself and Thorfinn. He seems to be interested but is unable to clearly communicate the nature of his interest since he's separated from his head. The Bus Came Back: After an extended absence, he reappears with his head in "Ghost Father of the Bride". Demoted to Extra: Unlike Sir Humphrey Bone, whose head is seen and heard regularly, Crash's head was missing for most of the first season. His head only appears briefly in the pilot but goes missing when Thorfinn accidentally knocks it off his neck. Only his headless body appears two more times in the rest of the first season, with his head never to be found, and it took until the NINETEENTH episode of season two for him to appear again (although at least it made him the star of said episode's B-plot, with his head finally being found). Afterward he's only been seen in only two episodes, the season 3 and 4 premieres, the latter of which has him missing his head again. Flat Character: He is the ghost we know the least about. We don't know how he died or what his life was like when he was alive, and there are barely any scenes between him interacting with the other ghosts. In fact, his appearances in seasons 3 and 4 were only out of necessity and a quick gag respectively.

characters.name: Stephanie
characters.description: '80s Hair: Stephanie, as an 80s mean girls based onHeather Chandlershe has a big, frizzy perm. Abhorrent Admirer: To Trevor, as she's still stuck in the body of a teenager. Alpha Bitch: Befitting her '80s "mean girl" image, she has not matured since her death and treats the ghosts and livings like various cliques she had in high school. She is resentful of Sam for trying to say the living had a bad prom experience making Stephanie show her mortal wound and calls Stephanie "creepy." Dick Dastardly Stops to Cheat: She had already convinced the other ghosts to come to her ghost prom and exclude Sam from it. When she tricks the ghosts into pulling a mean prank on Sam, all it does is convince them not to go to her prom. Evil Is Petty:She is more mean than evil, but she intentionally makes Sam relive a traumatizing memory. She did this either because Sam called her creepy, or because Trevor has a crush on Sam and not her (or maybe both).When she learns that Trevor is in a relationship with Hetty, she decides to try to break up all the heterosexual couples in Woodstone, because she doesn't want anyone to be in a relationship if she can't be.

characters.name: The Cholera Victim Ghosts
characters.description: Cargo Cult: Downplayed since they know it's an inanimate object, but as Sam put it "The water heater is basically their god." Creepy Basement: They reside in the mansion's creepy basement, and going down there creeps out even the upstairs ghosts. One of them even goes by the name "Creepy Dirk". Death-Based Ghost Powers: After dying of cholera, they gained the ghost power to give livings the symptoms of cholera. Did You Think They Can't Feel: Despite their kindness and patience, they're all a bit upset that the others consider themselves the main ghosts, and will quickly show that they have their own feelings. They're all upset after not being invited to Isaac's wedding, or if they feel they've been forgotten about or treated as less important. They're also quick to show grief when they believe Flower has been sucked off, due to her being the only "main Ghost" to genuinely befriend them and treat them as individuals. The Dog Bites Back: After enduring constant insults from the upstairs ghosts for all of Season 3 (and earlier), when Isaac doesn't invite them to his wedding, they lure him into the basement to be abducted by Patience. Downplayed, as they just thought Patience was going to yell at him, not drag him into the wall.

characters.name: Nancy
characters.description: Book Dumb: As expected given her lack of education in life. Taken up to eleven in series 3, where her failure to properly count the basement ghosts leads to the cast believing Flower has been sucked off, leading to her being stuck in a well for almost a year. A Day in the Limelight: When she accidentally possesses Sam in "A Very Arondekar Christmas", she is able to experience sensations she hadn't been able to since dying such as wearing shoes (since she’s barefooted) and tasting avocado. Things get complicated as she happens to possess Sam on the day Jay’s parents visit them. Death-Based Ghost Powers: Having died of cholera, she (like the other cholera ghosts) is able to give livings the symptoms of cholera. Did You Think I Can't Feel?: She's deeply hurt when Thor breaks up with her and insists that there were no feelings involved. Drop-In Character: She's by far the most prominent of the basement ghosts, and becomes a recurring character as the series goes on, eventually becoming a throuple with Flower and Thor.

characters.name: Nigel Chessum
characters.description: Accidental Murder: Nigel died when Isaac accidentally shot him in the chest while Isaac was spying on him with a looking glass attached to a musket and then sneezed causing the gun to go off. All Gays are Promiscuous: His comment on how he has a high body count heavily implies that he has never been in a proper romantic relationship before Isaac. Closet Key: It's Isaac's attraction to Nigel that finally forces him to admit he is gay after denying it for his whole life. Eternal Sexual Freedom: Despite being from the same era as Isaac, there's no evidence that he ever struggled with his sexuality. Incredibly Lame Fun: He finds watching ants to be an enjoyable and engrossing activity, considering he has little else to do as a spectre.

characters.name: Jenkins and Baxter
characters.description: Green-Eyed Monster: In "Farnsby & B" Jenkins tells Isaac that Nigel had been sucked off and Nigel that Isaac was dating Thorfinn out of jealousy of them possibly getting together. Then, in "Trevor's Body", Jenkins lets slip to the other ghosts that he and Nigel slept together on Christmas Eve when Nigel and Isaac were broken up to once again try to destroy their relationship. Instant Death Stab: Baxter seems to have died this way since he has a bayonet sticking out of him. Leitmotif: Courtesy of Baxter, a fife rendition of "The British Grenadiers." Satellite Character: Neither Jenkins nor Baxter seems to have much of a life outside of their service to Nigel (or in Jenkins' case, his one-sided crush on Nigel). In fact Baxter didn’t appear at all in season two.This gets lampshaded in the season 3 finale when Sam calls Baxter “very peripheral.” This gets lampshaded in the season 3 finale when Sam calls Baxter “very peripheral.”

characters.name: Molly
characters.description: MacGuffin: Jay let a neighbor borrow Molly's feather duster to use as part of a Halloween costume. He then had to get it back because the ritual that summoned her couldn't be reversed without it. One-Shot Character: She is accidentally summoned from the afterlife on Halloween, and sent back the same night. Sexual Extortion: She had sex with Elias because she knew he would fire her if she refused. Her husband was dead and she had a child to care for. You Are a Credit to Your Race: Hetty is amazedthat the only Irish person she ever bothered to get to know is the only good one.

characters.name: Luela and Flat Maria
characters.description: Body Horror: Flat Maria got her nickname because she died in a cattle stampede. Decomposite Character: They take on Annie's role as the house ghosts that moved on before Sam and Jay arrived. The Ghost: In addition to being literal ghosts, they have never been seen, only mentioned. They moved on to the afterlife sometime before Hetty's death. Named After the Injury: Flat Maria was trampled to death by cattle and flattened in the process, earning her the nickname she’s known by.

characters.name: Patience
characters.description: Adaptational Jerkass: Overall, Patience is far more mean and hateable than Annie ever was. Annie was a "true friend" to Mary and the other ghosts at Button House and while referring to Thomas like "a silly arse" and didn't appear overly impressed by him, she did feel sorry when he died and was missed by Mary when she moved on. Patience proves so obnoxious to everyone else that they can't stand her and were so happy when she announces she's decided to return to the dirt. Annie is aJerk with a Heart of Goldwho uses the rebellious girl visage to hide a nicer side, while Patience has a tendency to beJerk with a Heart of Jerkto manipulate others and is for the most rotten on the inside. Furthermore, Annie legitimately makes quite a few friends, while Patience drove away virtually all the other ghosts with her vileness and close-mindset. Adaptational Villainy: As much as Annie loves insulting the livings as a ghost with Mary, she never actually physically threatened or harmed the livings like Patience did. Adaptation Personality Change: Unlike Annie, Patience is far more committed to being a Puritan and is extremely strict and obsessed with being moral, where as Annie was more rebellious in life and didn't care that much about "committing sin/temptations" in death. And I Must Scream: She has spent over a hundred years wandering underground. When she finally appears, it's shown that her mental state has deteriorated and she's just a few steps away from being completely feral. Subverted when Isaac and Sass admit, she was pretty much the same before she went into the dirt. Then it's revealed that she wasn’t even alone the entire time, as she had befriended the bunker ghosts. Antiquated Linguistics: Unlike the other ghosts, who speak in mostly modern vernacular, she uses17th-century speech patterns with plenty of "dost"s, "thou"s, and "thee"s.

characters.name: The Bunker Ghosts
characters.description: Cargo Cult: They believe in a meteorite sent from heaven that only talks to their leader. Its actually just a hide-a-key rock. Good Is Dumb: They are nice and friendly enough that they can even get along with Patience. They are also dumb enough to fall for Bruce's crap.

characters.name: Bruce (né Jason)
characters.description: Hippie Name: He would rename the female members with these types of names. Hoist by His Own Petard: He was planning to force Flower into the dirt as punishment for telling an undercover cop about the cult. When he's exposed as a fraud, the cult forces him into the dirt. Obviously Evil: His act is not very convincing to anyone with a shred of sanity. Unnecessary Makeover:In-Universe. No one can figure out why he changed his name from Jason to Bruce.Pete: Jason to Bruce seems like a lateral move. You Are Number Six: Bruce made the male members go by "Male Number X".

characters.name: Eugene Woodstone
characters.description: Black Sheep/White Sheep: Zigzagged. The Woodstones considered him the black sheep of the family because he was weird and wanted to become a magician instead of joining the family business. However, since the Woodstones are a bunch of criminals and jerks, he is really more of a white sheep. Misplaced Retribution: He wanted revenge on his family for the way they treated him, but he tried to get his revenge by sabotaging Sam, who wasn’t even born until after he died.

characters.name: Judy Farnsby
characters.description: And I Must Scream:Played for Laughs. As she is unable to leave the property and can only be seen and heard by Sam, she is powerless to stop her son from hosting orgies and can only wait for them to be over. Housewife: She was apparently one in life as the outfit she's wearing as a ghost indicates. It's justified given she died at some point in the fifties when that would have been the most likely traditional gender role open to her. Laser-Guided Karma: She openly wonders if her lack of breastfeeding her son caused him to become the hidden swinger he is today. Tsundere: In "The Perfect Assistant" she has gotten to bullying Bjorn and causing her fellow ghost distress. At the end of the episode, Bjorn tells Thor after talking things out with Judy that she only bullies him because she has a crush on him.

characters.name: Bjorn
characters.description: Dating What Daddy Hates: He married a Danish woman and fathered three children with her, much to Thor's outrage. It isn't known if he knew his father would have been upset by this marriage. Missed Him by That Much: Towing the line between ironic comedy and genuine tragedy is the fact he and his father ended up as ghosts within shouting distance of one another when they died at separate times, yet they never managed to get close enough to see one another or interact for overa thousand years. Tragic Keepsake: When he died, he had the knife that Thorfinn left with him.

characters.name: George
characters.description: Armored Closet Gay: As a Puritan, he would have had to have been this when he was alive. Break the Believer: When a lifetime of following Puritan beliefs didn’t get him into heaven, he went in the extreme opposite direction and became all about sex all the time. Foil: Suprisingly, to Patience. While Patience was a dour, very miserable, cynical, true believer of Puritan beliefs who would rather be stuck in a endless hell in the dirt then change her lifestyle, and was even called too extreme by her fellow believers. George is far more laid back, believed he wasted his life by repressing his urges to fit in a religion that turned out to be false, changed in death drastically and when he's not talking about sex can be surpsingly nice and thoughtful. Bjorn at least doesn't seem to have a problem rooming with him. New England Puritan: Humorously averted as although he is dressed like one, he doesn’t behave like one. Isaac, the other gay ghost, finds him a bit too much for his liking. His out and proud behavior is from his beliefs and adherence to the lifestyle not getting him into heaven.

characters.name: Jessica
characters.description: The Alcoholic: All of her stories about her life involve drinking or being in a bar. She died driving drunk. Asian Airhead: She is Asian, and thanks to her perpetual buzz, not that bright. Bound to an Area: Zigzagged. She haunts a car instead of a house, so she can go to different places, but once there, she is stuck in a five-foot radius of the car. Did Not Get the Girl: After Freddie sells his car, Sam and Jay buy it back so Sass can still be with Jessica. However, since they already have a better car, the car that Jessica is bound to is mostly just going to sit in the yard. Since Sass can't ask her to be okay with being trapped in a 5-foot radius in the yard and she can't ask him to hang out with her in the yard for hours and miss stuff happening in the mansion, they decided to end their relationship and have Jay resell the car. Drunk Driver: Her true cause of death was that she was driving under the influence when she crashed into a telephone pole.

characters.name: Saul Henry
characters.description: Abhorrent Admirer: When Alberta first met him, the attraction was mutual. But once the novelty wore off, she found his constant attention to be too much. Bound to an Area: Zigzagged. He haunts a person instead of a house, so he can go to different places, but he is always stuck to being within a few feet of his current host. Discard and Draw: With some effort, he can remove himself from one person and onto another. After Alberta dumped him, he regrets trading Gene for Jay because Gene travelled to so many beautiful places. Jay helps him get back to Gene. Personality Powers: His clingy personality caused him to literally cling to people in the afterlife.

characters.name: Joan
characters.description: Abhorrent Admirer: A non-romantic version. Sam desperately wants Joan to take her under her wing as a writer. Joan seems to find Sam mildly annoying. Functional Addict: Joan started drinking at the age of eight and developed a tolerance by the time she was thirteen. She died with a flask on her so she kept on drinking even after death. She also smoked four cigarette packs a day. I Want My Beloved to Be Happy: After she develops feelings for Sass, she still supports his efforts to try to be with Shiki. Sweet and Sour Grapes: She supports Sass in his attempts to be with Shiki, only to have Sass realize he would rather be with Joan.

characters.name: Chris
characters.description: The Ever-Calm: Chris never freaks out, even when he dies and, as a ghost, watches a coroner zip his body into a body bag; his tone always remains even. However, this emotional detachment has consequences—he considers everything so casually that he doesn't bother thinking about Nigel, whom he had previously kissed, before leaving Woodstone. Mr. Fanservice:In-Universe. It's his job as a stripper. Why Do You Keep Changing Jobs?: He was the stripper at Isaac's bachelor party, then was the DJ at his wedding. Isaac took it as a sign that he shouldn't marry Nigel, Trevor said it was just a coincidence caused by the gig economy and a shallow suburban talent pool.

characters.name: Amunhotep
characters.description: And I Must Scream: Thanks to an incompetent untrained priest, his ghost was stuck inside his corpse, powerless and unable to move on or even talk to other ghosts, but still fully aware of what was happening around him. Superpower Lottery: Once released from his corpse, he is the most powerful ghost introduced thus far. Using ancient Egyptian curses, he can manipulate weather, summon locusts and rats to plague his victims, and reanimate his own mummy, controlling it to kill a target.

characters.name: Sheryl
characters.description: Ascend to a Higher Plane of Existence: Happens to her after she reconciles with Sam. Gasshole: Her ghost powers allow her to emit powerful shrimp burps on command. She uses it to let Jay know she's next to him when he asks. I Want My Beloved to Be Happy: Fakes this at first with Sam in "Sam's Mom", in an effort to get sucked off to the afterlife but has a real one at the end of the episode. Limited Social Circle: Because she's dead, she can only talk to the few other ghosts who hang around the restaurant, as well as her daughter. One-Shot Character: Introduced and written out within the space of a single episode.

characters.name: Shiki
characters.description: Childhood Friend Romance: Sass and Shiki knew each other since they were kids. However, she doesn't appear to have realized Sass' feelings about her until Sam tells her centuries after their respective deaths. Did Not Get the Girl:In Season 2, Sass gets involved with Jessica showing he has accepted he cannot be with Shiki because neither of them are able to move out of their death location boundaries.Sam's ended up blurting out Sass's feelings in front of her (Back in Season 1), ending that potential correspondence awkwardly. In Season 2, Sass gets involved with Jessica showing he has accepted he cannot be with Shiki because neither of them are able to move out of their death location boundaries. Sam's ended up blurting out Sass's feelings in front of her (Back in Season 1), ending that potential correspondence awkwardly. The One That Got Away: Sass still carries a torch for her five hundred years after their respective deaths, and regrets not having made a move when he was alive.

characters.name: David Woodstone
characters.description: But I Can't Be Pregnant!!: A variation. David can't have a child because he was rendered sterile early in his 20's due to a prank gone wrong (one of his finance bros dared him to put his balls in steaming hot coffee cup). This is a plot point during a episode where a conman tries stealing the house, but can't because Sam is the only Woodstone descendant left, and tries to make up a lie that David had a illegitimate child. Hell of a Heaven: Inverted. Like all ghosts, he is stuck in the place he died. He died in a strip club and is actually surprised when Sam tells him it isn't heaven. It Runs in the Family: Like Elias and Hetty, he likes drugs and has very little regard for people he considers beneath himself. Also like Elias, he habitually harasses women. Karma Houdini: Was a huge jerk in life, and was never punished fornot calling an ambulance when Trevor died. He's the only ghost who is happy about where he is stuck forever. Nom de Mom: When Sophie died, Hetty introduced herself as her ancestor, establishing that Sophie is a Woodstone by birth. There was no mention of Sophie being a single mother, and the night Trevor died, David said his parents were out of town. Still, David has the last name Woodstone as well.It's later revealed that Sophie is his mothers sister ( Sam's "Great-Aunt")

characters.name: Background Ghosts
characters.description: Dead All Along: The doctor reveals this to Sam during her visit. Funny Background Event: Sometimes, their antics aren't even verbally acknowledged. You Can See Me?: The minor ghosts often react with surprise and excitement whenever Sam sees them.

characters.name: Thomas Woodstone
characters.description: Heroic Sacrifice: His mother pulled one for him, reasoning that with her death he'd be able to keep the money and the house. I Just Want to Be Loved: His reasoning for Alberta's murder is that he was obssesed with finally being loved by someone after his mother(Hetty's) suicide, so tried to get between her and Earl. When that didn't work he poisoned her and got away with it. Karma Houdini: Zig-zagged. He lived out his days without ever answering for Alberta's murder, but he would never find true love even after Alberta died, the one thing he always wanted. Small Role, Big Impact: Only appears in flashbacks in two episodes, but he is the one that killed Alberta.
